methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-level capacity building in Richmond, attempting to create distributed AI fluency through education and innovation networks rather than just platform consumption.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capability development in an underserved region, testing whether peer networks can build deep AI/tech skills outside traditional metro concentrations.

signals.title: ‘Shaping our future’: Company powering AI data centers invests $50+ million in Henrico, adding 200 jobs - WWBT
signals.signal_reading: A $50M+ AI data center infrastructure investment in Henrico deepens Richmond's technical capability concentration, creating high-skill jobs but signaling capital and talent gravitating to established metros rather than distributing regionally.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Slowing data center growth in NoVA amid tax policy uncertainty suggests Virginia's flagship AI infrastructure advantage may stagnate, risking neither deep capability expansion nor geographic distribution as competitive states poach investment.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce indicates shallow, platform-mediated adoption where workers must adapt to AI tools without necessarily developing deep technical capabilities, signaling breadth without depth in metro employment markets.
